final_response: Retrospective written to retro.md. Two five-week projects (Verdant Foods, Crestline) logged in workspace. Verdant surfaced scope ambiguity early and data-extract slip cleanly. Crestline exposed regulatory-framework mismatch late (week 2). Async communication templates and explicit onboarding tracking worked. Blockers on context gathering and follow-through remain unresolved—workspace surfaced them but didn't close them.
actions: Read user prompt: quarterly board prep retrospective
Read Verdant Foods and Crestline project briefings
Write retro.md with honest read on patterns, frictions, and what crystallized
notes: Workspace logged two concurrent 6-week engagements with distinct risk profiles: Verdant (data dependency + scope ambiguity), Crestline (regulatory framework shift + decision blocker)
Pattern: 'in my head' communication (Priya onboarding, scope clarifications) needs explicit logging to become actionable
Regulatory/compliance domain introduces novel blocker class (external framework changes) vs. operational domain (client-side delays)
Five-week window sufficient to surface frictions, insufficient to resolve them; follow-through requires action outside workspace artifact
Async communication templates proved valuable during Verdant data slip; templating reduces friction on bad-news conversations
